texto: As long as a man has, he must give. And when he has nothing more, he is free. This freedom is far nobler than the former giving, for he no longer gives in accident but in essence, and he no longer gives one gift, but all gifts, and he no longer gives to just one man, but to all men.
explicacao: Orientação direta de John Tauler sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “As long as a man has, he must give. And when he has nothing more, he is free. This freedom is far…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, freedom, gifts, útil em momentos de gifts.

frase_original: As long as a man has, he must give. And when he has nothing more, he is free. This freedom is far nobler than the former giving, for he no longer gives in accident but in essence, and he no longer gives one gift, but all gifts, and he no longer gives to just one man, but to all men.

texto: In the most intimate, hidden and innermost ground of the soul, God is always essentially, actively, and substantially present. Here the soul possesses everything by grace which God possesses by nature.
explicacao: Citação de John Tauler sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “In the most intimate, hidden and innermost ground of the soul, God is always essentially, activel…” articula reflexao, grace, possesses em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
